Naval ROTC

The Naval ROTC program’s purpose is to educate and train qualified young men and women for service as commissioned officers in the United States Navy and Marine Corps. It prepares college students for leadership and management positions

in an increasingly technical and demanding military environment. NROTC midshipmen are required to complete the naval science courses and attend weekly professional seminars. During the summer, NROTC midshipmen participate in active duty at sea or shore-based training cruises for approximately four weeks. For more information, visit navalrotc.umd.edu, or contact navalrotc@umd.edu or 301-314- 6289.
